sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution satisfying requirements of City Charter allowing the City of Berkeley
(“City”) to participate in HGACBuy contract bid procedures and authorizing the City
Manager to purchase two (2) 2025 Ford F-450 North Star Ambulance chassis with
Braun Northwest, Inc. in an amount not to exceed $430,000.00.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The purchase of two (2) 2025 Ford F-450 North Star ambulance chassis will not exceed
$430,000.00. This purchase total includes document fees, California tire fees and
delivery charges. Funding for this purchase is available in the FY 2025 baseline
Equipment Replacement Fund 671.
sections.current_situation:
This purchase will replace the chassis of two (2) ambulances Equipment ID 19001 and
19003 that are nearing the end of useful service life as front line, initial response
vehicles. Both vehicles were put in service in 2020 and are planned for replacement in
July 2026. The average time to remount and upfit and an ambulance is 12 plus months
which is why the purchase is being requested at this time.
The existing ambulance bodies are customized for optimal use, are in good repair and
will be remounted on new chassis. The practice of remounting ambulance bodies on
new chassis rather than purchasing new ambulances is an effective cost saving
measure as it will save over $100,000.
These vehicles are used 24/7 by the City’s Emergency Medical Services (EMS) Division
in the course of their job duties.
This purchase agreement will support the City’s Strategic Plan Goal of creating a
resilient, safe, connected, and prepared City.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

sections.background:
If a purchase request exceeds $100,000.00, the Department of Finance, General
Services Division solicits bids or “piggybacks” off competitively bid contracts to ensure
the City’s departments are receiving the best pricing.
The City of Berkeley has been a no-cost member of the Houston-Galveston Area
Council (HGACBuy), a Cooperative Purchasing Program that has offered inter-local
purchasing since 2008. HGACBuy provides procurement services that make the
government procurement process more efficient by establishing competitively priced
contracts for goods and services made available to local governments nationwide.
All products and services offered through HGACBuy have been subjected to a public
competitive bid process. On June 9, 2023, HGACBuy released a Request for Proposal
No. AM 10-23 for Ambulances, EMS and Other Special Service Vehicles. The
solicitation was nationally advertised for forty-eight days (48). On August 10, 2023,
twenty-three (23) proposals were submitted and reviewed. On October 1, 2023,
Houston-Galveston Area Council selected Braun Northwest, Inc. as the best responsive
and responsible proposers to meet the specifications, thusly awarding Contract No.
AM10-23
HGACBuy charges an order processing charge for each sale successfully completed
through their contract. For this purchase a flat fee of $4,000.00 will be paid directly to
HGACBuy by Braun Northwest, Inc.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
For fleet purchases, Public Works staff researches the feasibility and availability of fully
electric vehicles and hybrid-electric powertrains to support the City’s transition off fossil
fuels. Staff research found that there are no viable electric or hybrid electric ambulance
chassis for consideration. The Fire Department would like to transition its fleet of front-
line EMS vehicles to electric. This is likely to not be financially feasible until all
firehouses are remodeled/rebuilt to include the appropriate infrastructure and/or the
price of
sections.rationale:
The vehicles are in-service now and the chassis are past their useful life. These vehicle
bodies are in good repair and can be remounted on new chassis. A purchase order is
needed to purchase new chassis and transfer the current ambulance bodies.
Ambulances in good working condition are essential to respond, provide treatment and
transport for members of our community that experience medical emergencies.
sections.alternatives:
None. The purchase of new ambulances is cost prohibitive.

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
PURCHASE ORDER WITH BRAUN NORTHWEST, INC FOR TWO (2) 2025 FORD F-
450 NORTH START ENGINE REMOUNTS OF AMBULANCE BODIES TO NEW
CHASSIS
WHEREAS, this purchase will replace engine chassis that are nearing the end of their
useful service life as front line, initial response vehicles. Ambulances in good repair are
needed by the City of Berkeley Fire Department to provide ambulatory services; and
WHEREAS, City Charter, by resolution, authorize the City to purchase goods without
undergoing a competitive bid process if the City uses pricing obtained by another entity
through a competitive bid process; and
WHEREAS, On June 9, 2023, HGACBuy released a Request for Proposal No. AM 10-23
for Ambulances, EMS and Other Special Service Vehicles. The solicitation was nationally
advertised for forty-eight days (48). On August 10, 2023, twenty-three (23) proposals
were submitted and reviewed. On October 1, 2023, Houston-Galveston Area Council
selected Braun Northwest, Inc. as the best responsive and responsible proposers to meet
the specifications, thusly awarding Contract No. AM10-23; and
WHEREAS, funding in the amount of $430,000.00 is available in the FY 2025 baseline
budget in the Equipment Replacement Fund (671); and
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a purchase order for two (2) ambulance engine
remounts to new chassis in the amount not to exceed $430,000.00.
